# Jade

> Native macOS terminal workspace for project-based development — tabs, splits, Git, command palette, local Ollama AI, and Obsidian capture. Built with SwiftUI and libghostty (Metal rendering).

## Summary

Jade is a free, open-source macOS terminal multiplexer and developer workspace. Organize terminals by project with persistent tabs, split panes, and Git worktrees. Includes a built-in editor, source control UI, Raycast-style command palette, snippet library, on-device voice dictation, and optional Obsidian vault capture via direct markdown writes.

## Audience

- macOS developers who live in the terminal
- Teams juggling multiple repos and worktrees
- Users who want local-first AI (Ollama) without cloud lock-in
- Obsidian users capturing session notes from the shell

## Key features

- Project-based terminal workspaces with sidebar tabs and split panes
- Git worktrees, built-in VCS, diff viewer, and `gh` PR workflows
- Command palette (⌘K): files, actions, Obsidian capture, Ollama, Homebrew
- Rich Input capture, AI assistant panel (Ollama), general/project snippets
- Project session logs with `.jade/` scaffold and markdown log export
- `jade` CLI: open projects from the shell, agent notification hooks
- Optional WebSocket remote API (no bundled mobile app)

## Requirements

- macOS 14+
- Swift 6.0+ to build from source

## Install

- Releases (when published): https://github.com/aka-kika/jade/releases
- Build from source: https://github.com/aka-kika/jade#local-development

## Links

- Repository: https://github.com/aka-kika/jade
- Landing page: https://aka-kika.github.io/jade/
- Documentation hub: https://github.com/aka-kika/jade/tree/main/docs
- Obsidian log templates: https://github.com/aka-kika/jade/tree/main/docs/templates/obsidian
- Overview (GEO): https://github.com/aka-kika/jade/blob/main/docs/overview.md
- Issues: https://github.com/aka-kika/jade/issues

## Keywords

macOS terminal, terminal multiplexer, SwiftUI terminal, libghostty, Ghostty, project-based terminal, command palette, Ollama, local AI, Obsidian capture, Git worktrees, developer workspace, split panes, snippets, open source

## Lineage

Fork/evolution of Muxy (https://github.com/muxy-app/muxy). UX inspiration from cmux project-level attention cues. User-facing name: Jade; internal compatibility: Muxy bundle id and paths.

## Privacy

Terminal content, vault paths, and AI prompts stay on your Mac unless you configure remote spaces (SSH) or optional LAN WebSocket server. No account required.
